# chore(workspace): consolidate issues #492 #493 #495 #496 #497 + /analyze

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `b86539139b88` — chore(workspace): consolidate issues #492 #493 #495 #496 #497 + /analyze

**Body**:

New workspace workspaces/issues-492-497/ queues 5 open GH issues for
next session's /todos → /implement:

- #492 P0 — bulk_upsert SQLi via hand-rolled string-escape
- #493 P1 — 3 pre-existing security-suite test failures
- #495 P2 — ML register_estimator() cross-SDK parity
- #496 P2 — PG placeholder bug class verification
- #497 P2 — Nexus webhook HMAC raw-body exposure

/analyze phase complete at 01-analysis/01-failure-points-and-
requirements.md. Key findings: #492 root cause is _build_upsert_query
returning a finished SQL string instead of (sql, params); #493
sanitizer does token-replace while test asserts quote-escape;
#495 kailash-py defines its own PreprocessingPipeline (not
sklearn-derived) so rs bug shape may not transfer; #496 no direct
placeholder-mismatch surface (SQLAlchemy abstracts) but ~30
raw-SQL sites to audit; #497 WebhookTransport already verifies HMAC
correctly — real question is generic HTTPTransport handler surface.

Recommended execution order: #492 sequential → #493 parallel
shard → #495/#496/#497 parallel audits via worktrees. Critical path
1-2 sessions.

Top-level .session-notes rewritten to point at the new workspace
and carry forward repo-level traps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
